keyPoints: Provides a dedicated Data Governance platform engineered to a secure metadata management catalog designed to search, tag, and discover datasets across multiple analytics engines..
Integrates Data Catalog capabilities directly into multi-tier cloud architectures, serving as a key component of the Databases, Analytics, and Data Lakes portfolio.
Encourages standard interface structures (REST APIs, Client SDKs, or console controls) to facilitate rapid cloud resource provisioning.
Manages relational schemas, NoSQL documents, and column-family tables with built-in replication and backup options.
Supports decoupled storage and compute architectures to process petabytes of analytical data without performance interference.
Implements Point-in-Time Recovery (PITR) to restore transactional databases to any microsecond for disaster recovery.
Optimizes Data Catalog performance through automated platform scaling and resource balancing.
Enables resource segregation, logical naming conventions, and tag-based configuration policies for Data Catalog.
Integrates with VPC routing and private service access architectures to secure communication tunnels.
Governs access to Data Catalog using standard IAM roles (e.g. roles/datacatalog.admin and roles/datacatalog.viewer) for fine-grained permissions.
Supports audit logging to record administrative actions and verify API configuration modifications for compliance reports.
Secures operational payloads using encryption at rest (Google-managed or Customer-Managed Keys via KMS) and SSL/TLS in transit.
Offers regional and multi-regional configuration options, duplicating service state across multiple zones to sustain outage events.
Enforces API quotas and rate limits programmatically to prevent resource starvation and noisy-neighbor interference.
Provides service recovery tools, regular status logs, and high-availability SLAs matching enterprise uptime goals.
Billed based on resource metrics (e.g., operations count, active connections, or gigabytes stored) allowing cost optimization.
Integrates with Cloud Monitoring and Cloud Logging to enable real-time alerting, metrics tracking, and diagnostic dashboards.
Enables command line administration through the gcloud CLI tool, allowing pipeline automation and scripting.

scenarios.scenario: An enterprise wants to construct a secure architectural pattern involving Data Catalog that blocks public internet ingress and enforces strict access restrictions.
scenarios.solution: Deploy the Data Catalog resource inside a VPC Service Control perimeter, routing connections privately using Serverless VPC connectors or internal gateways, and assign permissions using IAM roles.

scenarios.scenario: A development team needs to build automated CI/CD configurations that configure and audit Data Catalog operations on a regular basis while tracking costs.
scenarios.solution: Use Cloud Build scripts executing 'gcloud' commands to provision the Data Catalog resources, enable Cloud Audit Logs, and configure budget notifications at the project folder level.

commands.description: Create or configure a new Data Catalog resource
commands.command:
gcloud datacatalog create prod-datacatalog-resource \
  --region=us-central1 \
  --description="Production Data Catalog Resource"

commands.description: List active Data Catalog instances and query configuration states
